extraction.one_line_summary: LEKNESSUND Andreas attacked with 18km to go from the break, taking 38-second stage win and yellow jersey by time-trialing solo through final climb while GC teams failed coordination.
tactical_patterns: LEKNESSUND attacked with 18km remaining when gap was 90 seconds, gaining 10 seconds on climb while peloton expected catch.
Ineos, Bora, Quick-Step coordination reduced 6-minute gap to 90 seconds but abandoned chase when GC threat neutralized by time.
DECLERCQ, PALZER, VAN BAARLE combination cut gap most effectively before multiple teams diluted chase effort approaching sprint finale.
Break TT specialists (LEKNESSUND, former national champion) survive better than climbers when pace lifts before final categorized ascent.

rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: solo time-trial strength at 18km mark surprised peloton, gained time on final climb when GC teams expected reverse
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: exceptional sprint form mistaking breakaway win for stage victory suggests Tour de France readiness
rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: neutral: defended yellow successfully by following wheels but contributed nothing to chase despite team responsibility
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: dropped on final climb, unable to contest sprint finish on moderate difficulty finale
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: eliminated on final climb despite sprint finish expected by peloton
rider_signals.DECLERCQ Tim: positive: bridged from patron role to effective gap-cutting alongside climbing domestiques on rolling terrain
rider_signals.SCHÄR Michael: positive: survived break selection when stronger climbers dropped, suggesting improved climbing form
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: third place after full day of racing and climbing indicates strong Tour preparation form
